note: Reviewer subagents were not callable from the agent context (no Task/SendMessage tool surfaced). The producing agent performed a structured self-review against the security-auditor methodology. This report should be re-run by the team lead with the actual dev-security-auditor subagent before merging.

owasp_coverage.A01_broken_access_control: n/a — no privilege boundaries crossed; the function operates on already-collected maps.
owasp_coverage.A03_injection: PID validation via strconv.Atoi + pid > 0 is the explicit security guard mandated by tech-spec. Invalid PIDs (non-numeric, zero, negative) are caught and never used as a map key or pathname. Although buildProcPidResult itself does not construct a path, the validation here aligns with the readProcPidStat(pid)/readProcPidIO(pid) callers in task 05; defense-in-depth.
owasp_coverage.A04_insecure_design: Defensive design: validPID flag short-circuits all procfs-derived cells; haveCPU/havePrevCPU/haveIO/havePrevIO guards prevent stale map lookups; itv > 0 and ticks > 0 and cpuCount > 0 guards prevent division by zero and division by negative.
owasp_coverage.A06_vulnerable_components: No new third-party dependencies introduced. database/sql, fmt, strconv, strings are stdlib.
owasp_coverage.A08_data_integrity: delta() returns 0 for non-monotonic counter (curr <= prev), protecting downstream rendering from negative percentages. Integer division in formatCPUTime uses int64 cast — no overflow up to 2^63 / 100 jiffies (more than enough for any plausible CPU time).
owasp_coverage.A09_logging_failures: No logging in this layer (consistent with sibling stat readers).

secret_scan: No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or credentials.

input_validation.pid: strconv.Atoi + pid > 0; rows with invalid PID still rendered but procfs columns blanked. strings.TrimSpace applied before Atoi to tolerate accidental whitespace from CSV-style activity rows.
input_validation.itv: Checked > 0 before division (both CPU rate and IO rate paths).
input_validation.ticks: Checked > 0 before CPU rate division. formatCPUTime requires positive ticks per its godoc; non-positive ticks would produce Inf/NaN — caller responsibility, not exploitable by user input.
input_validation.src_len: len(src) checked before indexing src[0] and src[6] to tolerate malformed activity rows.

denial_of_service: buildProcPidResult is O(activity.Nrows) with bounded per-row work (constant 17 sql.NullString allocations + a few strconv calls). Map lookups are O(1). No unbounded loops, no recursion.
